name: latex-posters
tagline: Generates LaTeX research posters (beamerposter/tikzposter/baposter) with AI-generated visuals, overflow checks, and print QC.
verdict: No malicious behavior found: no credential access, no piped remote execution, no hidden instructions, no destructive commands. Main issues are quality/hygiene, not safety: the file is ~1600 lines (3x the size budget) with heavy repetition, contains a self-contradictory visual-to-text ratio (60-70% vs 40-50%), and its core AI-visual workflow repeatedly invoke

highlights.summary: Workflow repeatedly shells out to `python scripts/generate_schematic.py` (e.g. lines 318-355, 474-487) dozens of times as the core visual-generation step, but this SKILL.md-only review has no visibili
No 'when NOT to use' guidance is given anywhere in the file — e.g. no note that this isn't for slide decks, papers, or non-LaTeX posters.

Self-contradictory visual/text ratio: line 37 states 'Target: 60-70% of poster area should be AI-generated visuals, 30-40% text' while line 637 later recommends 'Balance text with visual content (40-5
